theme: cells that sort into a chosen pattern
title: Frustrated Adhesion: Sorting Cells Into a Chosen Ring Pattern
strategies: do-nothing baseline: J = 0 everywhere (no adhesion preference, dynamics never moves a cell, arrangement stays at its random initial state) -- reproduces the checker's own internal baseline

like-likes-like (diagonal-dominant): J[a][a] = -Jmax (maximally sticky same-type), J[a][b] = +Jmax for a!=b (maximally repulsive cross-type), used unconditionally regardless of the requested target topology -- the classical Steinberg differential-adhesion intuition; this nails engulfment/blob targets but actively drives AWAY from interleaved targets, since it maximizes same-type clustering when the target wants the opposite -- the trap

target-aware frustration search: read target_type, then simulate (using the SAME deterministic swap dynamics the checker uses) a small menu of candidate matrices spanning both signs and magnitudes -- diagonal-dominant, fully frustrated (off-diagonal favored over diagonal, the counter-intuitive choice), and half-strength variants of each -- and submit whichever candidate's simulated final arrangement gets closest to the requested topology; this exploits that non-blob (interleaved) targets are only stable minima under frustrated adhesion, the opposite of the intuitive like-likes-like rule
